comments.body:
**Bug: query param mismatch — validation view can never render**

`CKycRecordInfo` checks `record_verification` to decide which child to show:
```tsx
const forRecordVerification = searchParams.get('record_verification') === 'true';
```
But `CKycRecordOtpVerification` (line 49) navigates with `?record_validation=true` — a different param name. No code ever sets `record_verification=true`, so after OTP success the user is sent back to the OTP screen in a loop instead of the validation view.

Fix: use the same param name in both places.

**Missing API functions — build will fail**

`resendCKycRecordDownloadOtp` and `downloadCKycRecord` are imported from `@wallet/api` (`src/api.ts`), but neither function exists there. `activateWalletWithCkycInfo` (imported in `CKycRecordValidation.tsx`) is also absent.

These are defined in the `ckyc/04-shared-components` branch but that branch has not been merged into `feat/temp-master`. Also missing from the base branch: `AadhaarErrorBottomSheet`, `SvgContainer`, and `useAadhaarErrorHandler`.

This PR should not be merged until its prerequisites are in the base branch, or the required code should be included here.

**Naming: `isdownloadCKycRecordLoading` should be `isDownloadCKycRecordLoading`**

The destructured alias starts with a lowercase letter after `is`, breaking camelCase convention. Update the alias and all its references (lines 123, 144, 147).

**Naming: `isresendOTPLoading` should be `isResendOTPLoading`**

Same issue — lowercase `r` after `is`. Update the alias and all references (lines 109, 123, 144).

**Duplicate `otp_rate_limit_exceeded` message uses inconsistent string literal**

The `downloadCKycRecord` handler (line 66) writes the rate-limit message as a double-quoted string with a straight apostrophe. This handler uses a single-quoted string with a Unicode curly apostrophe (U+2019), producing two distinct string values for the same error.

Extract to a shared constant so both handlers stay in sync:
```ts
const OTP_RATE_LIMIT_MESSAGE =
  "You've entered the wrong OTP too many times. Please try again in 30 minutes";
```

**`snapPoints={[1, 1, 1]}` — all three snap points are identical**

A `BottomSheet` with three identical snap points at `1` (100% height) behaves the same as a single snap point. Use `snapPoints={[1]}` if the intent is a full-height fixed sheet, or distinct values (e.g. `[0.3, 0.6, 1]`) if multiple snap positions are wanted.
